project_goal: Build a lean, local-first AI project control cockpit that reduces owner babysitting.

owner_decisions.decision: Reducing owner babysitting is the primary rule.
Project Control Cockpit is local-first by default.
Project Control Cockpit V1 should not depend on direct paid API calls.
Claude Code is the initial worker layer for V1.
Advisor / verifier is a first-class role.
Worker claims are evidence, not truth.
The canonical project name is Project Control Cockpit. PCC is the approved short form.
Claude Code is the primary worker execution layer for PCC V1. Codex serves as the advisor/verifier and source-of-truth guard.
PCC must use explicit repo governance for canonical docs, idea intake, task promotion, and stale-doc handling. Ideas remain separate from active tasks.
Every meaningful change must trigger review and update of the relevant truth surfaces.
State consistency must be checked with a local deterministic validator before state updates are treated as complete.
A verified PASS advances the active task directly to complete rather than to an intermediate verified_pass state.
Worker-facing standing truth used by directive generation must live in canonical repo state rather than as hidden hardcoded script facts.
Fresh advisor and worker sessions must be restart-safe from canonical repo truth so chats are disposable rather than critical state containers.

Claude Code may temporarily hold both the worker and advisor/verifier roles, as an explicit, disclosed, owner-approved exception to DECISION-012, with independent re-checking and honest FAIL/INSUFFICIENT willingness required.

Verifier close-out includes running doctor/validate-cockpit-state, refreshing the advisor brief if stale, archiving cycle artifacts, and committing verified work to git. Pushing to any remote requires separate explicit owner approval each time.
V1 is complete as of pcc-v1-015; the project moves into BRR (Babysitter-Reduced Role) Phase 1, with reducing owner babysitting as the named top priority.

The BRR program plan is recorded canonically at docs/BRR_PLAN.md; Phase 1 is structured as four bounded, docs-only pcc-brr1 policy tasks (Owner Review Matrix, Task Safety Classification, Stop-Instead-of-Guess Policy, Operating Definitions), recommended for independent secondary review.
PCC returns to the canonical two-role split of DECISION-012: Claude Code is worker, Codex is advisor/verifier. The temporary dual-role trial (DECISION-019) ends and is superseded.
BRR Phase 1 policy scope is complete; PCC moves into BRR Phase 2, beginning with lightweight Task Classification Fielding as pcc-brr2-001.

Codex is available again. The DECISION-033 degraded self-verification fallback ends per its own terms; PCC returns to the canonical two-role split of DECISION-012/DECISION-023 (Claude Code worker, Codex advisor/verifier). A file-based polling bridge between the two is being scoped as a separate task.

active_constraints: State updates require verifier PASS or explicit owner override.
Prefer local deterministic tools before model usage.
Avoid fake intelligence scoring and fake truth detection.
worker_context_facts: Worker claims are evidence, not truth.
Claude Code is ready and pointed at this repository workspace.
PCC owns the worker handoff contract through repo files; the owner should not need to restate the instructions manually.
next_expected_action: Task 'pcc-brr5-005' is complete and verified PASS. Owner/advisor selects and drafts the next bounded task.
